title: Algorithms and Data Structures Recognition Quiz

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Queue" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Queue: A collection where the earliest added item is removed first
wrongAnswers: Depth-first search: A graph search that explores one path deeply before backtracking
Dynamic programming: A method that reuses solutions to overlapping subproblems
Stable sort: A sort that keeps equal items in their original relative order

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Hash table" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Hash table: A structure that maps keys to buckets for fast lookup
wrongAnswers: Breadth-first search: A graph search that explores nearby vertices before moving farther away
Greedy algorithm: A method that chooses the best immediate option at each step
Topological sort: An ordering of dependent tasks so prerequisites come first

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Binary tree" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Binary tree: A tree where each node has at most two children
wrongAnswers: Binary search: A search method that repeatedly narrows a sorted range
Backtracking: A method that tries choices and abandons paths that cannot succeed
Array: A sequence of values stored so each item can be accessed by position

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Heap" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Heap: A tree-based structure that keeps the highest or lowest priority item easy to remove
wrongAnswers: Merge sort: A sorting method that sorts smaller parts and combines them
Memoization: Storing function results so repeated calls can be answered quickly
Linked list: A chain of nodes where each node points to another node

listOfQuestions.question: Which statement about "Graph" is correct?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Graph: A set of vertices connected by edges
wrongAnswers: Quick sort: A sorting method that partitions data around a pivot
Big O notation: A way to describe how resource use grows with input size
Stack: A collection where the most recently added item is removed first
